LAW AND MOTION 301, JAN-24-2025 RE: DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS' ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF ALL CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING, AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312, AND MUST EMAIL IT TO CONTESTDEPT301TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. COURT REPORTER: GAIL DAVIDSON; CSR: 12823; T: (310) 488-8573; E: CALLMEGRS@EARTHLINK.NET. (301/HEK)

DEFENDANTS SAM WHEELER, ELLIOTT ENCARNACION, AMERICAN GUILD OF MUSICAL ARTISTS, INC., NED HANLON, SALLY MOUZON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TENTATIVE RULING PART 1 OF 2)
Off Calendar Jan-24-2025 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANTS SAM WHEELER , AN INDIVIDUAL, ELLIOTT ENCARNACION, AMERICAN GUILD OF MUSICAL ARTISTS, INC., NED HANLON, SALLY MOUZON DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Leonard H. Sansanowicz, Esq., Law Group of, (818) 639-8510, for Plaintiff.
Eileen B. Goldsmith, Esq., of Altshuler Berzon LLP, (415) 421-7151, and Eyad Asad, Esq., of Cohen Weiss and Simon LLP, (212) 356-0249, for Defendants.

The matter is ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants' alternative motion to compel arbitration of all causes of action in the first amended complaint is granted, the entirety of this case is stayed pending conclusion of the arbitration proceedings, and defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action in the first amended complaint is taken off calendar as moot. Defendants' combined demurrer and motion to compel arbitration and alternative motion to compel arbitration is an unauthorized effort to seek a ruling on their various requests for relief in their desired order. The court declines defendants' request to rule on their demurrer to the second cause of action in the first amended complaint prior to ruling on their alternative request that the second cause of action be compelled to arbitration. Paragraph 15 of the parties' separation agreement requires the parties to arbitrate "all {covered] disputes" which includes the second cause of action, so it is violative of that paragraph for the court to rule on the demurrer to the second cause of action because, as stated below, the second cause of action is a dispute that is required to be arbitrated by the separation agreement. The separation agreement requires the parties to arbitrate "all disputes arising out of or relating to" the separation agreement. (Separation Agreement 15). The subject matter of the separation agreement is the termination of plaintiff Nora Heiber's employment with AGMA including what AGMA may say to others about the termination of that employment. (Separation Agreement 8(b), (e) and (f)). Both causes of action alleged in the second amended complaint arise out of and relate to the termination of Ms. Heiber's employment including the propriety of what AGMA has allegedly said about the termination of Ms. Heiber's employment and thus those causes of action are required to be arbitrated by the plain language of the separation agreement. Ms. Heiber's argument that her claims against the individual defendants are not arbitrable per the separation agreement lacks merit. Defendants correctly cite California case law that non-signatories to an arbitration agreement are covered by the agreement where, as here, the non-signatories are alleged to have acted as agents of a signatory of the agreement.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, and in compliance with CRC 3.1312, and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Gail Davidson; CSR: 12823; T: (310) 488-8573; E: callmegrs@earthlink.net. (301/HK)

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT AMERICAN GUILD OF MUSICAL ARTISTS, INC., NED HANLON, AND SALLY MOUZON's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff's counsel requests the court to clarify the tentative ruling. The Court clarifies that the prospective employer issue was the point lacking in the complaint. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants American Guild of Musical Artists, Inc. (AGMA), Ned Hanlon, and Sally Mouzon's demurrer to the complaint is overruled in part and sustained in part with leave to amend.

Order signed (see signed order/word minutes for complete ruling).
